2606.20287.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
2606.20287.paper_summary: Effective Automated Essay Scoring (AES) are expected to support both reliable assessment and actionable instructional feedback. However, existing approaches often treat scoring and feedback as separate components: neural scoring models provide limited interpretability, while Large Language Model (LLM)-based feedback is typically insensitive to learners proficiency levels. To address this fragmentation, this work proposes PsyScore, a psychometrically-aware framework that integrates diagnostic assessment with instructional scaffolding through a shared latent ability representation. PsyScore comprises three key modules: a Trait-Adaptive Neural IRT Scorer that incorporates the Graded Partial Credit Model (GPCM) into a neural architecture, enabling the precise estimation of student ability while maintaining psychometric interpretability, a ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback Generator, which conditions multi-agent feedback strategies on the diagnosed ability parameter to adapt instructional focus across different proficiency levels, and a Multi-Perspective Feedback Evaluation Strategy that assesses feedback quality via pairwise preference judgements and student revision simulations. Experiments on the ASAP++ dataset demonstrate that PsyScore achieves competitive scoring performance while providing more pedagogically aligned feedback.
2606.20287.paper_summary_zh: 有效的自動化論文評分（AES）預期能支持可靠的評估和可行的教學反饋。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常將評分和反饋視為分開的組件：神經評分模型提供的可解釋性有限，而基於大型語言模型（LLM）的反饋通常對學習者的熟練程度不敏感。<lb/>為了解決這種碎片化問題，本研究提出了PsyScore，一個具有心理測量意識的框架，通過共享的潛在能力表示將診斷評估與教學支架整合在一起。<lb/>PsyScore包含三個關鍵模塊：一個特徵自適應神經IRT評分器，將分級部分信用模型（GPCM）納入神經架構中，能夠精確估計學生能力，同時保持心理測量的可解釋性；一個ZPD支架反饋生成器，根據診斷的能力參數調整多代理反饋策略，以適應不同熟練程度的教學重點；以及一個多角度反饋評估策略，通過成對偏好判斷和學生修訂模擬來評估反饋質量。<lb/>在ASAP++數據集上的實驗表明，PsyScore在提供更符合教學需求的反饋的同時，實現了具有競爭力的評分表現。
2606.20287.author: Wei Xia et.al.
2606.20287.authors: Wei Xia, Jin Wu, Haoran Shi, Xiangyu Wang, Chanjin Zheng
